sections.description: Platform-specific installation scripts that build the Go binary and register it as a system daemon on macOS (launchd) and Linux (systemd).
sections.purpose: Provides one-command installation for lil-olt-metrics on macOS and Linux. Each script builds the binary from source, installs it to /usr/local/bin, writes a default config if absent, and registers the service with the platform's init system so it starts automatically on boot.
strengths: Both scripts use `set -euo pipefail` for strict error handling
Preflight checks validate OS, root privileges, repository root, and Go availability before proceeding
Idempotent: existing daemons are stopped/unloaded before reinstalling, and existing configs are preserved
Linux script creates a dedicated system user/group (`lom`) and applies systemd hardening (NoNewPrivileges, ProtectSystem, ProtectHome, PrivateTmp)
Consistent structure and logging between both scripts makes them easy to compare and maintain
Usage/help flag supported in both scripts
weaknesses: No uninstall/remove mode — users must manually reverse the installation steps
No version flag or version tracking in the installed binary path
Darwin script does not create a dedicated user — the daemon runs as root via launchd
No rollback on partial failure (e.g., binary installed but service registration fails)
Config path is hardcoded; no flag to override install locations
No checksum or signature verification of the built binary
sections.test_coverage: No tests. These are operational scripts without automated test coverage.
dependencies: Go toolchain (required to build the binary from source)
Root/sudo privileges on the target system
Repository source tree with go.mod at the root and cmd/server as the build entry point
functionality: Build the lil-olt-metrics binary from `./cmd/server` using `go build`
Create data and config directories with appropriate ownership
Write a default `config.yaml` if one does not already exist
macOS: generate and load a launchd plist at `/Library/LaunchDaemons/com.bobcob7.lil-olt-metrics.plist` with KeepAlive and RunAtLoad
Linux: create a dedicated `lom` system user/group, generate a hardened systemd unit, enable and start the service
Gracefully stop/unload any previously installed instance before reinstalling
sections.files:
| File | Description |
|---|---|
| install-darwin.sh | Builds and installs lil-olt-metrics as a macOS launchd daemon under /Library/LaunchDaemons |
| install-linux.sh | Builds and installs lil-olt-metrics as a Linux systemd service with a dedicated system user and hardened unit file |
